name: Debt Hero: Chicago Debt Consolidation
description_short: Avant offers fixed-rate debt consolidation loans ranging from $2,000-$35,000 with APRs from 9.95%-35.99% and next-business-day funding for borrowers looking to simplify multiple debts into one monthly payment.
description_long:
Avant is a Chicago-based personal loan lender (222 W Merchandise Mart Plaza, Suite 900, Chicago, IL 60654) that specializes in debt consolidation loans through its parent company WebBank. The company has positioned itself as a streamlined alternative to traditional bank lending, focusing on the consumer pain point of managing multiple credit card payments simultaneously.

Avant's core offering is unsecured personal loans specifically designed for debt consolidation. Loan amounts range from $2,000 to $35,000 with APR ranges of 9.95% to 35.99% and flexible terms spanning 24 to 60 months. The company charges an administration fee up to 9.99% (deducted from loan proceeds), plus a $25 late fee if payment is 10+ days overdue and a $15 dishonored payment fee. They emphasize a streamlined application process with eligibility checks that don't impact credit scores, decisions within minutes, and next-business-day funding if approved.

Avant differentiates itself through speed, convenience, and customer service messaging. The company highlights "the personal touch" with real customer support, a mobile app for account management, and educational content through their Avant blog covering debt consolidation strategies. Their marketing emphasizes psychological benefits—converting "stressful debt into one calm monthly payment" and the sense of financial control gained through simplified budgeting. They serve multiple states with a detailed dropdown selector and provide state-specific rate and term information.

Honestly assessed, Avant is a mainstream personal loan lender with competitive positioning in the debt consolidation market but not necessarily lower-cost than alternatives. The APR range (9.95%-35.99%) is broad, meaning higher-risk applicants pay substantially more. Late fees and dishonored payment fees add costs for borrowers who struggle with payments. The administration fee up to 9.99% is a meaningful upfront cost. For borrowers with fair to good credit seeking convenience and psychological simplification of debt, Avant is a legitimate option; for those seeking the absolute lowest rates or smallest loans under $2,000, better alternatives exist.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Next-business-day funding if approved, addressing urgent debt consolidation needs
Eligibility checks that don't affect credit score, allowing rate exploration without hard inquiry damage
Flexible loan terms from 24-60 months, enabling customization of monthly payment size
Wide loan range ($2,000-$35,000) covering common consolidation scenarios
Fixed-rate loans eliminate payment uncertainty and simplify budgeting vs. variable-rate alternatives
Mobile app for account management and convenient payment tracking
Fast decision timeline (decisions within minutes of application)
Multiple state availability with transparent state-specific rate and term information
cons: APR range of 9.95%-35.99% is very broad, meaning subprime borrowers pay substantially higher rates than prime borrowers
Administration fee up to 9.99% is a meaningful upfront cost deducted from loan proceeds, effectively reducing net funding
Minimum loan amount of $2,000 excludes consumers needing smaller consolidation loans or emergency cash
Late fees ($25) and dishonored payment fees ($15) create additional costs for borrowers facing payment difficulties
Actual rates and terms vary by credit profile and state law, so advertised ranges don't guarantee individual qualification
best_for: Borrowers with fair to good credit seeking to consolidate $2,000-$35,000 in multiple credit card debts into one monthly payment
Consumers who value quick approval (within minutes) and next-business-day funding over shopping for the absolute lowest rate
Multi-state borrowers seeking a mainstream lender with consistent availability and transparent rate/term information by state
Individuals who benefit psychologically from simplifying multiple bills into a single fixed monthly payment for easier budgeting
diagnosis: Avant is best for borrowers with fair to good credit seeking a convenient, fast debt consolidation loan in the $2,000-$35,000 range who value next-business-day funding and simplified monthly payments over hunting for the absolute lowest APR. The main caveat is that actual rates depend heavily on credit profile—the 9.95%-35.99% range means risky borrowers will pay significantly more—and the up-front administration fee (up to 9.99%) and late/dishonored payment fees add real costs beyond the stated APR.
services: Fixed-rate loan products with terms from 24-60 months
Quick eligibility assessment without credit score impact
Fast loan decisions (within minutes of application)
Mobile app for account management and payment tracking
Customer support via phone and online channels
Educational content and debt consolidation guidance via Avant blog
State-specific rate and term information lookup
Administration fee structure (up to 9.99% of loan amount)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Avant offers fixed-rate debt consolidation loans from $2,000–$35,000 with APRs 9.95%–35.99% and next-business-day funding.
